blockedAssertions.blockedBy: No trustworthy desktop-shell or user-surface evidence proving tab reuse.
No trustworthy user-surface evidence for dirty indicator and clean-after-save behavior.
No trustworthy evidence for dirty-tab close confirmation UX.
Rename/move parity lacks committed regression coverage and desktop validation per scrutiny.
Create-if-missing write path is tested, but explorer insertion/openability was not validated on the user surface.
Delete/removal convergence across tree and editors lacks trustworthy user-surface validation.
No direct proof that diff view remains read-only and Accept/Discard produce the correct final state.
Neighbor-tab selection after close lacks committed regression coverage and desktop validation.
Scrutiny still reports incomplete dirty-buffer reload/revert safety despite partial targeted coverage.
Binary/media preview-mode behavior lacks trustworthy automated or desktop validation evidence.
Strict tool-router tests exist, but scrutiny reports assistant-side legacy browser attachment/runtime paths are still incompletely retired or not trustworthily evidenced.
No trustworthy user-surface evidence proves browser panel removal from shared app chrome.
Browser/CDP retirement is not fully proven on assistant/runtime surfaces according to scrutiny.
Scrutiny still reports unresolved document-core regressions, so non-browser surface health after browser removal is not proven.

notes: Project guidance and AGENTS.md explicitly avoid worker-run Tauri desktop validation by default and treat localhost:1420 as an internal helper, not a supported browser validation surface.
Automated validators were run successfully (npm run check, npm run test), but only VAL-EDITOR-007 had sufficiently direct automated evidence for user-visible behavior.

orchestratorOverride.reason: User explicitly waived user-testing/manual desktop validation as a mission gate. The orchestrator is overriding this failed user-testing result so implementation can continue based on automated validators and implementation evidence.
